name: Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus
name_original: Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro – circular desde Graus
region_intro: Hiking around Graus offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Spanish Pyrenees foothills, transitioning from lush western Pyrenees scenery to more arid, rocky terrain. The region is characterized by high mountain peaks, limestone and granite formations, and the picturesque Ésera River valley. Proximity to the Sierra de Guara Natural Park provides unique geological formations, including canyons and gorges, alongside varied ecosystems. Best hiking trails around Graus The most popular hiking route is Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This easy route passes historical chapels and offers views of the surrounding area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Grustán and the Hermitages of Graus Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail explores more historical hermitages and the local countryside. Local hikers also love the Plaza Mayor de Graus – Ermita de San Miguel loop from Graus, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through the town's surroundings and towards the Ermita de San Miguel, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Graus is defined by Pyrenean foothills, the Ésera River valley, and the limestone canyons of the Sierra de Guara. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Graus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Graus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect when hiking around Graus?
A: Hiking around Graus offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from the lush green of the western Pyrenees foothills to more arid, rocky terrain. You'll encounter high mountain peaks, dramatic limestone and granite formations, and the picturesque Ésera River valley. The nearby Sierra de Guara Natural Park features unique geological formations, including impressive canyons and gorges sculpted by water.

Q: Are there hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Graus?
A: Yes, Graus offers a good selection of trails for various skill levels. There are &lt;strong&gt;over 10 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4 km trail that passes historical chapels and offers rewarding views.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Graus area?
A: Yes, many routes around Graus are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938983315/grustan-and-the-hermitages-of-graus-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grustán and the Hermitages of Graus Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical hermitages, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Puente de Abajo loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.2 km walk.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can discover on hikes near Graus?
A: The region boasts spectacular natural features. You can enjoy sweeping views of valleys and majestic mountains like the Turbón in the Pyrenean foothills. The Ésera River valley offers glimpses of the Pic de Aneto and Maladeta Massif. Within the Sierra de Guara Natural Park, you'll find impressive cliffs, clear rivers, and unique karst landscapes, with panoramic views from the Tozal de Guara.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical and cultural elements. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt; lead past historical chapels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938983315/grustan-and-the-hermitages-of-graus-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grustán and the Hermitages of Graus Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores more historical hermitages. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption of Mediano&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samitier Castle&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Graus?
A: Graus is suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, especially in shaded areas or along rivers. While the high Pyrenees might have snow in winter, many lower-elevation trails in the foothills and Sierra de Guara remain accessible, offering a different, quieter experience.

Q: How long do hikes around Graus typically take?
A: Hikes around Graus vary in duration. Easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Moderate trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Graus – Ermita de San Miguel loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2 hours 15 minutes. Longer moderate routes can extend to 3-4 hours.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs accessible near Graus for hikers?
A: Yes, the region around Graus features several beautiful lakes and reservoirs. You can find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4990014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Barasona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Torreciudad Sanctuary and the Grado I reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight in the area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Graus?
A: The trails in Graus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from the Pyrenean foothills to the unique canyons of Sierra de Guara, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels. The blend of natural beauty and historical sites is also frequently highlighted.

Q: How many hiking trails are available around Graus?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 25 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; available around Graus, offering a wide range of experiences. This includes 12 easy routes and 16 moderate routes, ensuring options for every preference and ability.

Q: Is it possible to find more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While the immediate Graus area offers mostly easy to moderate trails, its proximity to the Pyrenees provides access to more challenging terrain. For experienced hikers, the GR11 (Senda Pirenaica) long-distance trail, which traverses the Spanish Pyrenees, passes through the wider region, offering rugged sections with significant elevation gains and outstanding landscape diversity.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest in the Sierra de Guara Natural Park accessible from Graus?
A: Yes, Graus's proximity to the Sierra de Guara Natural Park is a major draw. This park is famous for its unique geological formations, including limestone canyons and gorges. Hikers can explore fascinating terrain like the Mascún Route with its Mallos de Rodellar, or the accessible Alquézar Footbridges route in the Vero Canyon. The ascent to Tozal de Guara, the highest peak in the park, provides panoramic 360-degree views.
tips: The Basilica of the Virgen de la Peña is, without a doubt, the most emblematic building in the city of Graus. As its name indicates, the basilica stands on a hill guarded by a great rock wall. The current temple was built in the 16th century, although there was already a Romanesque sanctuary before. It is well worth visiting the cloister of the basilica, from where you have fantastic views of Graus and the surroundings. In summer, classical music concerts are also held in the cloister.
There is a via ferrata that passes under this viewpoint, the Peña del Morral via ferrata.
Good views of the Ésera and Isábena valleys from here!
